title: Health | Beehive.govt.nz
content:
## Rural health roadshow lands in Te Kūiti
Associate Health Minister with responsibility for Rural Health Matt Doocey and Minister for Rural Communities Mark Patterson are in Te Kūiti today as part of the Rural Health Roadshow.

## Pharmac saves money to spend on new medicines
Associate Health Minister with responsibility for Pharmac David Seymour, and Health Minister Simeon Brown are pleased to see Pharmac negotiating savings to increase medicines access.

## First Rural Training Hub coming to South Taranaki
Associate Health Minister with responsibility for Rural Health Matt Doocey and Minister for Rural Communities Mark Patterson, are in Hāwera today for the ninth stop on the Rural Health Roadshow, where it has been announced that the country’s first Rural Training Hub will be in South Taranaki.

## $20 million to boost ED staffing
Emergency departments will receive a $20 million boost to increase frontline staffing across hospitals nationwide over the next nine months, Health Minister Simeon Brown has announced.

## $100 million boost to upgrade hospitals nationwide
The Government is delivering a major $100 million investment to accelerate improvements across New Zealand’s hospital facilities, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Work ramps up on Nelson Hospital Redevelopment
Enabling works are now in full swing for the Nelson Hospital Redevelopment, with the site being prepared for major construction, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## New Dunedin Hospital construction contract signed
Health Minister Simeon Brown has today announced that the contract for the construction of Dunedin Hospital’s new inpatient building has been signed with CPB Contractors Limited.

## ASMS union walks away from patients
Today’s decision by the Association of Salaried Medical Specialists (ASMS) union to walk away from bargaining and continue with strike action next week will leave more than 13,000 patients without the care they need.

## Nurse-led cystoscopy clinics delivering timely cancer care in Dunedin
A new nurse-led cystoscopy service at Dunedin Hospital is helping bladder cancer patients receive their essential follow-up care on time, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## New hub will bring outpatient services together in Christchurch
The refurbishment of Christchurch Hospital’s new Ambulatory Care Hub is now complete, with services beginning to transition into the new space, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Upgraded dental unit opens in Whanganui
Patients attending Whanganui Hospital’s onsite dental unit will now benefit from a modernised and upgraded facility following a refurbishment, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Health workforce regulations to put patients first
Patients will get faster access to care under new changes to modernise health workforce regulation, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Expanded NICU to support more babies in Christchurch
A $13.9 million investment to upgrade and expand the Neonatal Intensive Care Unit at Christchurch Women’s Hospital will provide a significant boost to neonatal care services for families across Canterbury and th

## Digital health service delivering faster access to primary care
More than 21,000 consultations have already been delivered through the Government’s new 24/7 digital health service launched in July, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Alcohol levy funds new investment in FASD
Associate Health Minister Matt Doocey today launched the Government’s new approach to Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD), bringing a significant funding boost and valuable action to further prevent FASD.

## More Kiwis receiving surgery as Elective Boost exceeds first target
The Government has exceeded its first Elective Boost target, delivering more than 16,000 additional procedures so far and reducing wait times for patients, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## More Aucklanders getting faster access to heart specialists
Fewer people in Auckland are waiting to see a cardiologist, with the cardiology first specialist assessment (FSA) waitlist down 25 per cent since November 2023, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Binding arbitration sought to resolve senior doctors’ dispute
Health Minister Simeon Brown has today written to Health New Zealand and the Association of Salaried Medical Specialists (ASMS) calling on both parties to urgently enter binding arbitration.

## NZNO union strike to disrupt more than 13,000 surgeries and appointments
Thousands of New Zealanders who are already waiting too long for care will now wait even longer because the New Zealand Nurses Organisation (NZNO) union has decided to strike again this week.

## Whangārei Hospital parking expansion shifts into gear
Patients and families visiting Whangārei Hospital are one step closer to easier access, as a procurement process begins for additional and upgraded carparks to support the hospital’s ongoing redevelopment.

## Emergency services receive cellular location capability boost
New Zealand’s emergency services are getting digital technology that will rapidly speed up the process for locating people at risk, say Police Minister Mark Mitchell and Associate Health Minister Casey Costello.

## Medsafe consents improve year on year, again
Annual performance statistics released today show Medsafe following my direction and approving new medicines faster than last year.

## Faster, more accurate brain tumour diagnoses for Kiwis
More than 100 New Zealanders have already benefited from a groundbreaking diagnostic tool that is delivering faster, more accurate brain tumour diagnoses, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Midwives to play greater role in immunisation efforts
Community midwives will now be funded to deliver antenatal immunisations to pregnant women, Health Minister Simeon Brown announced today.
